title: Account of Shabib's End
description: According to Hishim- Abu Mikhnaf-Abu Yazid al-Saksaki: It was al- Hajjij himself who had sent us out against him462-that is, Shabib- and now he distributed a great deal of money among us, giving a share to every man who had been wounded or shown particular valor. Then he ordered Sufyin b. al-Abrad to march out against Shabib, and Sufyin prepared to do so. This distressed Habib b. 'Abd al-Rahman al-Hakami, who said, "You send Sufyin against a man whom I routed and whose best horsemen I killed!" Two months later, however, Sufyin carried out his orders.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 22
